Title: Two distinct domains contribute to the substrate acyl chain length selectivity of plant acyl-ACP thioesterase

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: FatB acyl-ACP thioesterase; two hotdog domains
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Type II fatty acid synthase terminal release step
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Free medium-chain fatty acids, mainly C8 and C10 fatty acids
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes the thioester bond of acyl-ACP intermediates to release free fatty acids and ACP, terminating type II fatty acid synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Culture medium of engineered E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Energy-dense molecules useful as biofuel and bioproduct precursors
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of predominantly C8/C10 free fatty acids and produces about threefold higher fatty acid titer than CvFatB2 when expressed in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CvFatB1 is a subclass III FatB thioesterase that preferentially hydrolyzes C8/C10 acyl-ACPs. 2. In E. coli expression assays, it generated a medium-chain fatty acid profile distinct from CvFatB2. 3. CvFatB1 sequence served as the donor of key residues that, when introduced into CvFatB2, shifted CvFatB2 toward CvFatB1-like substrate specificity and higher productivity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in E. coli K27; GC-MS metabolite profiling of free fatty acids; domain swapping; site-directed mutagenesis; immunoblotting
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced from lacZ promoter by IPTG in E. coli K27
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: E. coli K27 fadD88 background; M9 minimal medium with glucose; IPTG induction; 30 °C cultivation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering microbial or plant systems to produce medium-chain fatty acids; provides sequence features for tailoring chain-length output toward biofuel-oriented products
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Source gene was assayed as a codon-optimized mature peptide lacking the plastid transit peptide in E. coli. Although a plant enzyme, the metabolic effect was evaluated in bacteria.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: FatB acyl-ACP thioesterase; two hotdog domains
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Type II fatty acid synthase terminal release step
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Free long-chain fatty acids, mainly C14 and C16 fatty acids
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes acyl-ACP intermediates to release free fatty acids and ACP, terminating type II fatty acid synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Culture medium of engineered E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Energy-dense molecules useful as biofuel and bioproduct precursors
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of predominantly C14/C16 free fatty acids in E. coli; specific residues V194, V217, N223, R226, R227, and I268 determine this selectivity, while L257, I260, and L289 enhance catalytic activity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CvFatB2 is a subclass I FatB thioesterase preferring C14/C16 acyl-ACPs. 2. Domain swapping mapped major specificity determinants to N-terminal fragments, especially fragment III. 3. Site-directed mutagenesis identified V194, V217, N223, R226, R227, and I268 as primary determinants of chain-length selectivity and L257, I260, and L289 as activity-enhancing residues linked to ACP-interacting positive patches. 4. Combining 11 substitutions converted CvFatB2 to a CvFatB1-like enzyme and also generated synthetic variants with even shorter-chain specificity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in E. coli K27; GC-MS metabolite profiling; domain swapping; site-directed mutagenesis; structural modeling; western blotting
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced from lacZ promoter by IPTG in E. coli K27
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: E. coli K27 fadD88 background; M9 minimal medium with glucose; IPTG induction; 30 °C cultivation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key enzyme scaffold for rational redesign of chain-length specificity and fatty acid productivity in engineered microbes or crops; useful for tailored fatty acid production
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper’s central mechanistic conclusions are built mainly on CvFatB2 mutagenesis. Two distinct structural regions control function: an acyl-binding cavity in the N-terminal hotdog domain and positively charged surface patches likely involved in ACP binding.
